beginner.question: What does display: flex do on a container?
beginner.answer: display: flex creates a block-level flex container, turning all direct children into flex items.

beginner.question: What does flex-direction control?
beginner.answer: flex-direction controls the direction of the main axis, determining whether items are placed in a row or column.

beginner.question: What does flex-wrap do?
beginner.answer: flex-wrap controls whether flex items wrap to a new line when they overflow the container.

beginner.question: What does flex-flow do?
beginner.answer: flex-flow is shorthand for flex-direction and flex-wrap combined, e.g., flex-flow: row wrap.

beginner.question: What is the default value of flex-direction?
What is the default value of flex-wrap?
What is the default value of flex-flow?
How does flex-direction: column affect items?
beginner.answer: Items are arranged vertically from top to bottom instead of horizontally.

beginner.question: How does flex-wrap: wrap affect items?
beginner.answer: Items that overflow the container width wrap to the next row instead of shrinking.

beginner.question: What does flex-flow: column wrap do?
beginner.answer: It arranges items vertically (column) and allows them to wrap into additional columns.

intermediate.question: How does flex-direction: row-reverse affect item order?
intermediate.answer: Items are placed in reverse order, from right to left (or left to right in RTL).

intermediate.question: How does flex-direction: column-reverse affect item order?
intermediate.answer: Items are placed from bottom to top in reverse order.

intermediate.question: How does flex-wrap: wrap-reverse work?
intermediate.answer: Items wrap in reverse order, creating new rows above instead of below the first row.

intermediate.question: What happens when flex-wrap: nowrap and items overflow?
intermediate.answer: Items either shrink (flex-shrink applies) or overflow the container horizontally.

intermediate.question: How do you create a centered row with flex container properties?
intermediate.answer: Use display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center on the container.

intermediate.question: How do you make flex items wrap at specific breakpoints?
intermediate.answer: Combine flex-wrap: wrap with flex-basis or width values that trigger wrapping when space runs out.

intermediate.question: What is the interaction between flex-direction and justify-content?
intermediate.answer: justify-content always aligns along the main axis, so it changes direction when flex-direction changes.

intermediate.question: What is the interaction between flex-direction and align-items?
intermediate.answer: align-items always aligns along the cross axis, perpendicular to flex-direction.

intermediate.question: How does the gap property work in a flex container?
intermediate.answer: gap adds spacing between flex items only, not before the first or after the last item.

intermediate.question: Can you set different row-gap and column-gap on a flex container?
intermediate.answer: Yes, use row-gap and column-gap separately, or gap shorthand (gap: row column).

advanced.question: How does flex-direction: column affect percentage-based width on flex items?
advanced.answer: In column direction, percentage widths refer to the container width, not the column height; heights are content-based.

advanced.question: How does the flex container's min-height affect column-direction items?
advanced.answer: flex-direction: column with min-height on the container gives items a defined main axis size to reference.

advanced.question: How does flex-wrap interact with align-content?
advanced.answer: align-content distributes space between wrapped rows/columns; it only has an effect when flex-wrap is enabled.

advanced.question: How do you create a multi-column layout with flex-flow: column wrap?
advanced.answer: Set a fixed height on the container and use flex-flow: column wrap; items will fill columns top to bottom.

advanced.question: How does block-direction wrapping differ in RTL writing modes?
advanced.answer: In RTL, row-reverse and wrap direction are mirrored; items flow right-to-left and wrap accordingly.

advanced.question: How does the flex container manage its own sizing?
advanced.answer: A flex container sizes based on its content by default, but respects width, height, and explicit sizing.

advanced.question: How do you create a horizontal menu with flex container properties that wraps responsively?
advanced.answer: Use display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: center; gap: 10px; on the nav container.

advanced.question: What happens to the flex container when all items have flex: 0?
advanced.answer: Items do not grow or shrink, so the container maintains its content-based size and items do not overflow.

advanced.question: How does the flex container handle overflow when flex-direction: column and no height is set?
advanced.answer: Without a defined height, the container expands to contain all items vertically; overflow does not occur.

advanced.question: How does `container-type: inline-size` interact with flex containers in container queries?
advanced.answer: A flex container can be a query container, allowing children to respond to the container's inline size rather than the viewport.
